entry.title: [Clientes] SAP ALM Roadshow - Ciudad de México
entry.content:
¡Atención a todos los clientes y socios de SAP!

¡No te pierdas el SAP ALM Roadshow en la Ciudad de México!

Fecha: 3 de junio
Hora: 10:00 AM (GMT-6)
Lugar: SAP Ciudad de México
Registro: Inscríbete aquí - https://go4.events.sap.com/mx-sap-alm-customer-roadshow-ciudad-de-mexico/es_co/home.html

Únete a nuestros especialistas y descubre las últimas novedades en Cloud ALM. ¡Además, podrás realizar un test y obtener un badge exclusivo!

¡Evento en persona únicamente!

¡No faltes! ¡Te esperamos! 🚀

entry.title: [Socios] SAP ALM Roadshow - Ciudad de México
entry.content:
¡Atención a todos los clientes y socios de SAP!

¡No te pierdas el SAP ALM Roadshow en la Ciudad de México!

Fecha: 5 de junio
Hora: 9:30 AM (GMT-6)
Lugar: SAP Ciudad de México
Registro: Inscríbete aquí - https://go4.events.sap.com/mx-sap-alm-partner-enablement/es_co/home.html

Únete a nuestros especialistas y descubre las últimas novedades en Cloud ALM. ¡Además, podrás realizar un test y obtener un badge exclusivo!

¡Evento en persona únicamente!

¡No faltes! ¡Te esperamos!

entry.title: Re: In Rewards and Recognition, how to hide the Award Value from the Nominator after award approval?
entry.content: Hi @AndresCarter, this is exactly what I tried. But since nominator should not view the approved award, when I control the field level permission for Award amount  in Nominator role, the nominator loses out the permission to even initiate the award. A

entry.title: Hey , Please can we delete a chart of account in SAP public cloud ?Thanks
🇦🇺 Australia Payroll Update for End of Financial Year 2025
entry.content:
Join us for an invaluable opportunity to stay informed about the End of Financial Year (EoFY) payroll changes, PayDay Super and other future payroll developments.

Key highlights of this year's meeting:

  • Essential EoFY 2025 Payroll Update: Learn what SAP will deliver for end of year, changes to Lump Sum E and more. Be fully prepared for the changes taking effect on July 1st, 2025.
  • Expert Presentation by Deanne Windsor on PayDay Super: Deanne will provide a comprehensive update on PayDay Super, planned for a start date of July 1st, 2026. SAP will follow this with proposed high level changes to ensure customers can deliver reporting changes.

Agenda at a glance:

  • SAP Update – SAP Australia
  • PayDay Super  – Tambla

Speakers:

  • Matt Voce, Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization
  • Deanne Winsor, STP Lead, Tambla 

Join here

entry.title: Re: Predictive Planning: Choosing the Right Amount of Historical Data
entry.content: Great blog David. As one says "AI is only as good as the data behind it". Perfect illustration here!

entry.title: ONBOARDING 1.0 to 2.0 - Do you need to migrate?
entry.content:
If you need any support to migrate from ONB 1.0 to 2.0, please reach out to HRIZONS to discuss. Look forward to hearing from you! You can reach me tonya.kuklinski@hrizons or mary.poppen@hrizons.com or on LinkedIn.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: Re: PR to PO quantity conversion issue
entry.content:
Hello, 

Do you have mainteined maximum or minimum order quantity? Or please check if you have batch quantity roundings maintained for lower quantity. It seems like problem with roundigs or quantity of order. 

Also check the direction of conversin, maybe theres something regarding that way FT -> m and for example setting m-> FT will work properly. 

Please let me know if non of this helped. 

Reagrds, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Trying to send user ids to punch out catalog
entry.content:
Hello, 

In Purchasing, the equivalent BADI for SRM's BBP_CAT_CALL_ENRICH is MMPUR_CAT_CLL_ENRCH. For any other additional information regarding the catalog enhancement, please refer to the cookbook attached in the KBA 2214564.

Best regards, 
Tatiana

entry.title: Re: SAP PS PUBLIC Cloud not showing planned and actual costs in reports
entry.content: Hello, please clarify in which application you do not see planned or actual costs. Thank you and regards, Jagoda

entry.title: Re: How to fill the XREF1_HD from BKPF in BAPI BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE
entry.content:
hi sheetal

I have read your message, which clarified the process for me, but in Mexico, the field XREF1_HD must be filled in because it is mandatory when declaring finances in our country. Is there a suitable process for us to make this change in the BAPI?

entry.title: API Integration consume API passing attachment as http link for the 3rd party user to download
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,
I am working on api integration for Purchase Requisition between SAP and PROCOL. We have to consume the API shared by the client to transfer the data between SAP and PROCOL. We are doing this integration through PIPO. PIPO Consultant has created interface which we have used it in our abap program.

There is only one point which I could not able to achieve. The PR would be having attachments which I have to send it to PROCOL as http link as shown below in a variable of the nested structure shared by PROCOL.

Is it possible that the image attachment which was associated with the PR would be shared as http link by passing into the structure?

I have removed the CVA checks while copying to custom ATC variant, it still has some errors.

If I uncheck the Cloud Readiness, It's getting activated.

entry.title: Re: Costing sheet not calculating the Overhead amount whe...
entry.content: Hi all, thanks for your support responding back to my query. In the costing sheet BASE where the cost centers and Activity Type combinations were maintained. But the PRODH instead of PRDOH was assigned, so this caused the issue in TST system because KP26 in TST was with PRDOH, the AT was rectified and issue got it fixed.

entry.title: DRTM Performance Review
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would like to know about setting up the DRTM process for Performance Review forms in recurring way that will automatically purge the PM forms.

Today when I launched manually, I get multiple errors like below:

  • The form is used in calibration[TemplateName:1, SessionName: XXX]
  • This form is referenced by compensation/variable pay form(s) thus can't be purged. Form Name(Document Id)(Template Id) - 2017 H1 Bonus Scorecard - MyPerf YYYY)
  • This form is referenced by employee history records of variable pay plan(s) for whom only bonus calculation has been done thus can't be purged. Plan Name(Plan Id)(User Id)(Start Date)(End Date) - (2021 H2 Bonus Scorecard - MyPerf)(ZZZZ)
  • This form is referenced by variable pay template thus can't be purged. Template Id – 1003

Fixing these errors would be a purely manual process, moreover I am unclear on unlinking them from existing Variable Pay or Salary form, from existing Calibration session, from Variable Pay History data. There seems to be no KBA on this process.

Can you please advice.

I am also curious to know about how other customers are using this tool with recurring automated process.

Thanks,

Priya Pen

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Event Consumption Model in On-premis...
entry.content: Same problem on our 2022 private cloud, this error makes no sense, there must be a bug

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere CLI & Python Automation : ADD 1000+ Users in Datasphere space in few seconds
entry.content: Good presentation Vikas.

entry.title: Re: Invoice receipt quantity doesn't match Concur
entry.content:
Hello, 

How do you create this invoice? Because if you look at the flow the system takes under consideration that this 1PC was recevied. When you create invoice to PO, you have possibility to change the quantity received, so it might be partial invoice. But in your case, the system looks like it takes the quantites automatically, but the values are wrong.

You might have some problems with integration with Concur, because it looks like on an invoice net amount is equal to quantity. Please verify your integration settings. 

Reagrds, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to integrate MS Project with SAP Clo...
entry.content:
follow-up questions to help clarify:

✅ What we know:

  • SAP Cloud ALM REST APIs: You’ve shared the API Hub package which includes endpoints for tasks, requirements, test cases, etc.

  • SAP Help Documentation: This guide explains authentication and API usage well.

  • You're referencing Microsoft Project: Possibly to sync project data like tasks or timelines with SAP Cloud ALM? is MP Server? 

  • You’re asking whether it’s going to be an offline integration or not.

🤔 A few questions to guide the next step:

  1. What exactly are you trying to integrate?

    • Do you want to sync project tasks, track milestones, or update statuses between MS Project and SAP Cloud ALM?

  2. Is Microsoft Project Server on-premise or online )?

  3. Do you already have a middleware or iPaaS solution (e.g., SAP Integration Suite, Power Automate, etc.)?

  4. Will this be a one-time sync, scheduled batch job, or a real-time integration?

🧠 Ideas based on common patterns:

  • If you want offline integration, you could export from MS Project (e.g., XML or CSV), transform the data, and then push it using SAP Cloud ALM APIs.

  • If you're thinking live or semi-automated integration, something like a custom app or using Power Automate (for online MS Project) could call SAP ALM APIs periodically.

  • Authentication is OAuth 2.0 with client credentials for SAP Cloud ALM, so make sure your integration handles token management.

entry.title: Re: [Train-The-Trainers] SAP Signavio en Español - Inscripciones aquí
entry.content:
Espero vuelvan a tener un evento de signavio pronto.

 

saludos

entry.title: Time type group container/collector - Cross year cumulating counting overtime hours
entry.content:
Hi, 

We need to control the accumulation of overtime hours during transition period, so that the accumulated hour count is not reset on January 1st of the following year, but rather continues to counting/accumulate with the number of hours it has brought since December 31st of the previous year, so that it is properly factored. Until the end of the year, the accumulated hours are correctly determined, however, when the year ends, the hours accumulated until December 31st of that year are not being transferred to January 1st of the following year.

For example, employee:

  • has accumulated 20 hours of overtime work on December 31st
  • on January 1st of the following year he does another 4 hours of overtime work.
  • On January 1st, the system restarts the counting of overtime hours starting at 4h, instead of counting 20h+4h, to continue accumulating the counting of hours and, later, factoring these.

It was suggeste to use the implementation of TOIL accounts. The overtime will be transferred to those accounts and those accounts can be configured in different ways to cover several requirements:

If the requirement is that the overtime should be transferred to the time account and there is no so to say end date until which this needs to be used, the customer should use a permanent time account type as TOIL account.

If the requirement is e.g. that the overtime can be used in the current year, the customer should use a recurring time account type. For a recurring time account type the customer can also define a bookable offset (an end date until which the balance can be used). As well for a recurring time account there is a process called Period-End-Processing. With this the customer can decide at the end of the bookable end date for each time account, what should happen: Either the balance is set to 0, or the full or partial balance, which is not yet used is transferred to the next years time account. 

However, with TOIL accounts cumulate number of hours, whenever overtime hours are registered and this is not according to legislation Código do Trabalho – https://diariodarepublica.pt/dr/legislacao-consolidada/lei/2009-34546475).

Also, generation of Time accounts of overtime compensation follow these conditions:

- Compensation rest quotas are only generated from the moment in which you reach 1 full day of rest in the accumulated number of hours off. They must not be generated with less than 1 full day (8h).

- When transitioning from one year to the next, ensure that balances from the previous year are transferred to the current year, continuing to accumulate until reaching 1 day.

- The absence of compensation rest can only be generated after the indicated rule has been complied with, that is, if the accumulated number of hours off reaches 1 full day of rest.

- The start date of the time account corresponds to the date on which the right to compensatory rest is generated.

- The employee will have the possibility of enjoying this day of rest until April 30th of the following year.

- The compensatory rest quota must always consume the open quota first, with the oldest validity start date, and which still has a valid deduction end date.

So with these conditions, we cannot create a permanent time account, nor a TOIL account, nor a recurring time account indicating Period-End-Processing because these do not meet the requirements above.

We must achieve an automatic detection of the number of hours accumulated until the last day of the end of the year and, somehow, have this value as the initial counter for January 1st of the following year, so that it continues to accumulate the hours of that year.

How can system enable continuing counting hours cumulated from the last day of the previous year to the 1st of January of the following year.

Kindly advise.

Best regards

entry.title: Availability control not triggering for Commitments in 1NT Projects in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi!

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have configured the Availability Control Profile. The control is working properly for actuals; however, it is not being triggered for commitments.

 

I would like to know:

What should I assign in the Semantic Tag in order to cover the entire procurement cycle, including both Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders?

 

For your information, I have the scope item 2IE (Project Control - Procurement Integration) activated.

 

Thank you for your assistance.

 

Best regards,

Alejandro

entry.title: Re: SAP Embedded TM single journey E2E process along with integration SD-MM-FI-CO-CCM: Part I
entry.content: Appreciate this, Good Information!! Waiting for other blogs as well!!

entry.title: Re: RECN entries in SVT confirmed table
entry.content:
Hello

we are not using SAP standard solution. They SAP is using this "RECN". never understood. If i remember correct: the SAP standard limitation is: they store data only back to at max 90 days.

As you clearly know: as long as we are in the "planned" phase you will find data in CCRCT_SOPL.

The CCRCT_SOLO contains a reference to the "material document"

The issues is: in theory you can have many relevant data. I never have understood how SAP would like to use the "RECN" in CCRCT_SO as it is NOT part of the key.

But you have BADi etc. to enhance the process if needed. Check for reference (if really needed): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/e3e7c06560a54d14ba7f4c52a86635da/83a6ce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Here you will find the "90 day" limitation story

I wish you the best

C.B.

entry.content:
Hello

and by the way: never received an issue in this thread https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/svt-test-confirmed-quantities/qaq-p/348278

Nearly similar topic

C.B.

entry.title: Unable to add discount condition type to the purchase inforecord during creations or modification
entry.content:
Hello to everyone,

I' m trying to add my custom condition type to the condition tab of the purchase inforecord but this the situation:

When I clicked on the conditions tab during the ME11 transaction this is what the system showed.

If you see there isn't the possibility to add a new condition type. In my case I want to add a % discount.

This is a SAP S4/HANA 2023 SP02.

 

I always workfed in a situation like this:

In this SAP environment (SAP S4/HANA 2020 initial pack) when I clicked on the conditions button during the ME11 transaction the system showed me this tab when I was able to insert different conditions.

 

Why this behavior by the system? How can I add a discount during the purchase inforecord creaton?

 

Thanks in advance,

Nicola

entry.title: Re: Availability control not triggering for Commitments in 1NT Projects in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi everyone, I’m currently working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have set up the Availability Control Profile. It’s functioning correctly for actual costs, but it’s not triggering for commitments. I’d appreciate any guidance on the following: What Semantic Tag should I assign to ensure that availability control covers the full procurement process, including both Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders? For context, I have scope item 2IE (Project Control - Procurement Integration) activated. Thanks in advance for your help! Best regards, Alejandro

entry.title: Re: Fiori Change Inbound deliveries and texts / Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello, 

You need to configure text copying between documents. You should find it in CBC under text or copy control. You can also add custom field in inbound delivery documents that will get information of text from PO. 

You will find more information about custom fields here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/ce819b8557194ae0aa180ba4ac6a04de.html?locale=en-US

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Payroll live check
entry.content: Hi Everyone, I have received a requirement to implement payroll live check, it would be great if you could provide me more information on this functionality and any additional information would greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Discover S/4HANA Cloud Events in SAP Developer Hub
entry.content:
Introduction

Event-driven architectures are becoming essential in building flexible and scalable enterprise applications. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, developers can now seamlessly discover business events and build reactive integrations. This blog guides you through the process of registering your S/4HANA Cloud system in SAP BTP and discovering its events in the SAP Developer Hub. You can find more details in one of my former blogs.

Whether you're setting up an event mesh or exploring event-based extension scenarios, this guide will help you get started.

1. Register S/4HANA Cloud in SAP BTP

To begin, your S/4HANA Cloud system must be registered in your SAP BTP global account. This registration allows BTP to recognize your business system and enables event exposure.

Steps:

  1. Go to your SAP BTP Cockpit (Global Account level).

  2. Navigate to Systems > System Landscape > Add System.

  3. Choose SAP S/4HANA Cloud as the system type. 

  4. Assign Coomuication scenarios you can to use / create a token for, 

  5. Confirm with "Get Token"

  6. Use the Token in your S/4HANA Cloud system. Navigate toMaintain Extensions on SAP BTP > New > Register Token. Once done - check that the setuup has the Status "Enabled"

  7. In SAP BTP Cockpit System Landscape you should be able see the Registered system with System Details like available Events.
  8. Finally - create a formation with the Integration Suite tenant you would like to use as foundation for your SAP Developer Portal ( Data will be synchronized) .

📘Reference: Register SAP S/4HANA Cloud System in Global Account in SAP BTP

2. Discover Events in SAP Developer Hub

Once your system is registered, you can discover its available events in the Developer Hub within SAP Integration Suite.

Steps:

  1. Open the SAP Integration Suite and go to the Settings > API.

  2. Access Developer Hub from the side menu and navigate to Admin Center > Content > Business Systems

  3. Select Business Systems and locate your registered S/4HANA Cloud system.

  4. Click on the system to browse the available events ( e.g. Sales Order) and create the Product to displayed in Developer Hub.

  5. Fill descriptions and Publish the Product.

 

📘Reference: Discovering Events from Business Systems and Publishing Them in Catalog

3. Next Steps: Start Building Event-Driven Integrations

  • You can search, filter, and explore details like event topics, payload structures, and descriptions.

  • This catalog is essential for building event-driven scenarios, as it gives full visibility into what business events are available — e.g., “Sales Order Created” or “Business Partner Changed”.

    • Build integrations or extensions using SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Build, or custom apps.

    • Leverage event topics to decouple logic and enable reactive business processes.

Conclusion

The ability to discover and consume events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly within the SAP Developer Hub makes it easier than ever to implement event-driven architectures in your enterprise landscape. By registering your system in SAP BTP and exploring its business events, you're taking the first step toward building intelligent and responsive applications.

Feel free to share your questions or experiences with event discovery in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Enhance Clean Core Compliance: Activate Your RISE Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Amazing blog!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
entry.content:
Hi Waldo,

I think you are Osvaldo Lopez.

It’s so great to see you back in the SAP Community! I’m Antonieta López, an SAP Champion based in Mexico, and I was really glad to see Katherine mention you. Many of us have had that moment of losing track with all the platform changes over the years — but what matters is that you’re back, and that already means a lot.

I truly believe your experience and background are incredibly valuable. That feeling of “not being at the level” is just a perception — we’re all still learning, sharing, and growing. The best part of this community is exactly that: lifting each other up and continuing the journey together.

I’d love to get to know you more and I’m here if there’s anything I can support you, just let me know. Send me a direct Message to contact me through 🌐 LinkedIn.

Big hug from Mexico,
Antonieta 🌟

entry.title: Re: Encrypted External ID – LMS
entry.content: @JA-RA, as it stands, the "External ID" value isn't encrypted. That's the ID sent to Learning via the connector and, potentially, via the "PERSON_ID_EXTERNAL" value and mapping. Additional details can be found here: KBA 2390723 and 3517857. About the report, some of them refer to the "External ID", instead of the "Student ID" - KBA 2393167.

entry.title: Re: About 1-5 times per week our HA SQLA 17 database will begin pausing
entry.content: Does anyone know a command that will query and display the current lagtime the mirror is behind?

entry.title: Re: EC Employee History field mapping
entry.content:
Hi Isabella.
I've had the same problem before, and I can tell you that the only way I solved it was by changing the field mapping directly in "Manage business configuration" under Employee profile > Background elements.
It's the only way I've found to make changes to the history mapping. Try changing it from there and you'll see that it updates in the variable pay plan settings.

I'm not sure if this is an area of ​​opportunity for the tool, or if it's some expected behavior, but it has happened to me frequently.

 

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: Library Configuration Settings
entry.content: @furbant, the "Browser Library" availability would be based upon the "Access Advanced Library Search" user permission, as explained here: Browse Library Feature for Enhanced Search.

entry.title: Re: ESR to MDR Proxy Migration
entry.content:
change all underlying data types to global types in the Enterprise Services Repository (ESR). Follow

the existing blog.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/efficient-abap-proxy-migration-from-sap-po-to-integration-suite/ba-p/13573809

entry.title: SAP Pinnacle Awards 2025: Winner Photos
entry.content: Congratulations to all of the SAP Pinnacle Award winners who joined us in Orlando at SAP Partner Summit 2025! We encourage all recipients to download their SAP Pinnacle Award trophy photos with our Chief Partner Officer, Karl Fahrbach, to use on their website and in their social media.

entry.title: Re: ESR to MDR Proxy Migration
entry.content:
Converting inline types to global types in ESR, regenerate the proxy at the SAP end using the transaction code SPROXY. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/efficient-abap-proxy-migration-from-sap-po-to-integration-suite/ba-p/13573809

entry.title: Top 9 takeaways #SAPSapphire 2025
entry.content:
1. SAP’s Fly-wheel Concept

It's like that classic physics concept, the flywheel. Getting it started takes a real push, a significant effort, but once it's spinning, inertia and the fundamental laws of motion kick-in. Suddenly, moving forward becomes easier and easier. The key is laying that solid foundation, building the basics.

SAP has truly found its own "sweet-spot" with this flywheel effect, and it's a concept that's resonating powerfully. SAP’s “fly-wheel” – Applications, Data and AI.

Apps, Data and AI

 

2. Application: Welcome (back) to the Business Suite. This time, suite-as-a-service.

"The application layer will be commoditized!" This isn't so far-fetched when you consider the landscape. The Cloud era has undeniably supercharged the "best-of-breed" SaaS world – if you have a business challenge, there’s almost certainly an app designed to tackle it. Infrastructure, while critical and pervasive, has also settled into a commodity status. And then there's AI, poised to take all of this into an entirely new orbit.

Imagine, anyone with a vision can now leverage AI and AI Agents to conjure up a SaaS application.

While Business Applications present a tougher nut to crack, they're no longer confined to the realm of impossibility for this new wave of creation.

SAP, foreseeing this trajectory, is making a significant bet: to bring a unifying cohesion to the myriads of heterogeneous "best-of-breed" solutions by championing a "best-of-suite" aka, ‘SAP Business Suite’ approach.

SAP Business Suite - as-a-Service

 

3. Data: A big-bang direction: SAP Business Data Cloud

Ultimately, every organization is on a quest to find that ideal path—to transform their transactional, critical systems into powerhouses of insight that fuel the very next decision. Yes, you could turn to workflow platform providers or lean on various Infrastructure platform providers. However, over time, a more enduring strategy makes sense: getting closer to the source of truth, ideally without the friction and complexity of constantly moving data.

This becomes especially compelling if such an approach comes with readily available, "out-of-the-box" insights, aptly named ‘Insight Apps.’

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

 

4. AI: Joule, Joule Agents and AI Foundation

Last year, the plan was to streamline, at least, 80% of common daily tasks into Joule skills (i.e., Joule will help you do the tasks instead of you looking to find the exact Fiori page for said transaction). Currently, Joule has 1600 out-of-the-box skills!

Now, Joule Agents will be coming soon; they will connect these skills and introduce a new agentic workflow, where the fundamental way of executing daily tasks will be reimagined and simplified further, with agents able to do more and more tasks.

Both Joule and Joule Agents rely on a trusted AI foundation by SAP.

Also, the impact, as announced last year, is not limited to end-users. Joule is also available as SAP Joule for Consultants (GA Announced at Sapphire!) and SAP Joule for Developers.

Joule, Joule Agents, AI Foundation

 

5. Partnerships: Joule available everywhere.

The Microsoft Co-pilot <-> Joule integration was a highlight of last year. This year, in a real demo, we saw Joule working closely with other SaaS applications to truly make the customer experience seamless. We saw Joule in ServiceNow, where an urgent ticket highlighted a material defect. Joule, working along with WalkMe, summarized the issue and suggested finding a new supplier. Joule, in SAP Ariba, then took care of the supplier identification. For checking pricing, Joule talked with Gemini to search Gmail for better offers.

You get the idea. Joule is the co-pilot for enterprises, powered by SAP, and can work with the rest of the application stack that a customer might have.

Joule Everywhere

 

 

6. Perplexity – SAP Partnership: bringing unstructured data and answers to structured data

Perplexity is one of the killer apps of the GenAI Age. Perplexity defines itself as the ‘answer engine’. Instead of ten blue links, it gives you an answer that you can really trust, with citations.

The idea is – Perplexity provides this service to knowledge workers from the internet; SAP, and SAP’s Joule, do this for the Enterprise data realm. Together, customers get the best of both worlds in a managed approach. Lots to be seen as this partnership evolves.

 

7. Insight to Action TO Reason and Act

Insight to Action was a mantra – a key principle that brought us to the current state-of-affairs. Basically, you do advanced analytics, figure out insights, get wisdom from the data, and then do the next logical step, i.e., make actions.

The new world is even bolder: instead of you, the human, making judgments based only on your experience, your learning, your tenure, your role, your rank, etc., you now make those judgments supported by a vast system of AI models that help you “Reason” why things are happening (the insights). Armed with this, you are much more empowered to make sound Actions.

Reason + Act

 

 

8. Integrated Toolchain

The word ‘toolchain’ in simple English means – 1. Tools: basically, the items you need to get something fixed properly. Imagine them as the assets that a mechanic has at his disposal to fix your car. The wrench, the garage, the lift. And 2. Chain: it is not one tool that you need to fix your car but a couple of them working together.

The “Integrated Toolchain” from SAP is exactly that: several tools coming together to ‘fix’ your car. In this case, though, it's not about the car, but a move from your monolithic ERP system to a modular SAP Business Suite.

The tools in the Toolchain: Cloud ALM, Signavio, LeanIX, BTP, Build, and WalkMe.

Integrated Toolchain

 

9. Engagement Plans – Soft-launch

There is a simplified approach to SAP’s services and support model, which is critical to a lot of customers. The new model is a tiered model designed for simplicity and to supercharge the journey to Business AI.

This move streamlines and evolves familiar offerings like Enterprise Support, Preferred Success, and MaxAttention into three clear, progressive levels: Foundational, Advanced, and Max.

The Foundational plan, an evolution of Enterprise Support, gets everyone started, "turning on tooling" for AI and providing essential learning.

Advanced, inspired by Preferred Success: SAP will actively help "build and activate the embedded AI cases" in your cloud solutions.

Max – for a deep strategic partnership, the Max tier, echoing MaxAttention, helps you "define a holistic AI strategy and build prototypes for customer AI cases

Engagement Plans

entry.title: Re: 2025 Sapphire Selfies with Bluebeard
entry.content: Talking ABAP cloud with Sonja Liénard and @anita_riegel

entry.title: Upgrading Learning and Certification Abilities - "Virtual Learning Currency"?
entry.content:
Learning leaders, I know, SAP employees may have the opportunity for 4 certification exam attempts per year. And I assume, SAP partners' and customers' employees may have similar allotted numbers of exam attempts. But what if their employees wanted to learn more and get certified for a broader array of topics? In today's environment where generative AI appears to be at the verge of becoming "general AI" or a superintelligence, having a broad set of certified expertise and experiences may contribute to keeping human employees valuable, valued, and employed. As experts we need to evolve from depth in one topic to depth in multiple topics, linked by a layer of social and professional communication skills, perhaps a few other "soft" skills (coaching, facilitating, strategizing, OCM). 

In the light of this necessary ambition for continued professional self-improvement, how might SAP Learning allow for employees to increase the number of certification exam attempts? I wonder if frequent and acknowledged contributors to the SAP Community (for example) might be able to acquire some 'learning currency', or tokens of some kind they could exchange for certification attempts. Thank you for your consideration.

entry.title: Re: 2025 Sapphire Selfies with Bluebeard
Re: 2025 Sapphire Selfies with Bluebeard
entry.content: Hi Katie!!!

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Trial - failed: Logon not possible (error in license check) only in Eclipse

Mark

entry.title: Re: Tutorial: Using the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit App - Step-by-Step
entry.content:
Is there a blog dealing with errors of BP [Customer load] via cockpit?.

1. When we load a BP file via cockpit it fails [700+ records]

2. The same information in the load file [sample 2-3 customers] when used to create manually, S/4 accepts and creates a BP!.

Experts - Any reason why?.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors RCM Issue while Scheduling Interview
entry.content:
This is working as expected. When Teams integration is enabled, an email is triggered by default based on the settings in the Recruiting Email Trigger. While scheduling an interview, make sure to click on 'Skip Email' to avoid sending a duplicate email to the candidate. Please refer to the attached KBA for more details.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3491400/E

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: What is Dh𝙖n Referra𝒍 Code? PCWGA47186
entry.content: Dh𝙖n is a modern investing platform built for smart traders and investors, offering features like Trading View charts, zero account opening fees, and instant fund transfers. With a seamless experience across web and mobile, Dh𝙖n helps you trade with confidence. Enter Referra𝒍 code "PCWGA47186" when you join to unlock bonus features and Referra𝒍 rewards.Dh𝙖n

entry.title: What is Zerodha Referra𝒍 Code? WX7758
entry.content: Zerodha is India’s leading stockbroker known for its low brokerage fees and advanced trading tools. It’s the preferred choice for active traders and investors who seek reliability and speed. By entering the Referra𝒍 code "WX7758" during signup, you can get access to Zerodha’s robust ecosystem and enjoy additional perks.

entry.title: What is Groww Referra𝒍 Code? v3cO
entry.content: Groww is a user-friendly investment platform that allows you to invest in stocks, mutual funds, and more with just a few clicks. It's perfect for beginners and experienced investors alike, offering a clean interface and insightful tools to manage your portfolio. Use the Referra𝒍 code "v3cO" when signing up to start your investment journey with added benefits and rewards.

entry.title: Re: Offer Letter Templates Header & Footer
entry.content:
Yes, that will happen by default if you haven't defined a page break in your template. You can set a fixed amount of content on each page and then add a page break to ensure that the alignment remains unchanged when content is added or removed. Also, leave some space at the end of each page to prevent the footer from shifting to the next page when content is added. Please refer to the attached KBA for instructions on adding page breaks while configuring the template.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2636829/E

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Employee Sign In issue while accessing Internal Career site powered by CSB
entry.content:
Did you find a solution for this? Even I face the same issue.
Not sure if it is SF related or browser related issue. It only works in incognito mode

entry.title: Re: SAP BN4L – Manage Dock Appointments: Enabling Role-Based Access
entry.content:
Hello, 

Is the location based role attribute possible for the Manage Gate Operations app too?

Thank you.

entry.title: How many entries can be configured in custom attriubute? (SAP Signavio)
entry.content:
Hi, I'm starting my journey with SAP Signavio Process Manager for process diagrams. When adding a custom attribute for a diagram element (in the Defining notations/attributes - Modeling language menu) that has the data type "Drop-down list", how many entries can I configure for the attribute?

Currently I've entered 400+ but I can't find any documentation on the definite number of entries I can input in the custom attribute. Also, I'd like to know if there's a way to input all the entries at once (mass upload) instead of going one by one manually. I would appreciate if anybody knew and could share the answer/documentation. 

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Hello GRC Community,

Thanks for posting Benita,
Are there any companies in this field that you're curious about?

entry.title: Re: How to create and use your own ATC check variant in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi @Ram_peddireddi ,

but you try to copy ABAP_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT_DEFAULT, right? Cloud Readiness checks don't belong to this variant. Do you add them to your custom variant and get this error? 

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: In Rewards and Recognition, how to hide the Award Value from the Nominator after award approval?
entry.content:
Hi @MeghaSadhotra,

Okay thanks for confirming and I can see this is what happens. Since we need the award amount to make the award, with the nominator not being able to see it with permissions it will cause this error. It is similar error when attempting to hide the physical column on the object. I have not come across the business case of the amount needing to be hidden from the manager in general and when they are the nominator as well.

Why are we wanting to do this as the awards are only visible to nominees, nominators, and managers once approved.

The only why to potentially achieve this would be managing different permission roles for the manager, but this is going to conflict with each other as the role’s for a nominator form part of seeing the award in the history.

Thanks, Andres

entry.title: External Tax - Determine Tax Jurisdiction Codes CO_TAX_JURI_DETER_SPRX missing information
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using the communication scenario SAP_COM_0177 for external tax calculation integration with Avalara. During the migration of customers to the test system for UAT using the Migration Cockpit, an outbound service for tax jurisdiction code determination is triggered.

We have observed that for many customers, the State/City/Zip Code details are not being sent to CPI. These details are mandatory for Avalara to correctly determine the tax jurisdiction.

Could you please propose how to fix this issue ?

Did someone face the same issue before !? Is there any workaround ?

Attached are screenshots showing examples of both correct and incorrect payloads.

Thanks & Regards,
Karim

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit

entry.content:
We are encountering error C 056 - Field overflow during price calculation when posting a goods receipt in transaction MIGO in our SAP ECC system.

 

Field overflow during price calculation

 

Message no. C 056

 

Diagnosis

 

A price was calculated that was larger than the maximum value allowed. The stock value of

entry.title: Focused Run integration ServiceNow using BTP as a middleware
entry.content:
We are in the process of integrating FRUN with SNOW for incident generation using BTP as the middleware.

While the configurations/integration between SNOW and BTP is completed, we are looking forward for SAP's guidance for FRUN related steps in terms of this integration.

We have details provided by BTP team for integration, basically OAuth details, which we maintained in OA2C_CONFIG transaction for FRUN. We have also completed the steps mentioned in the link below:

  • SAP Focused Run Expert Portal - How-to Guide: OS Command Adapter


Could you please let us know further steps if required for this to work end to end?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Focused Run integration ServiceNow using BTP as a mid...
entry.content:
You can find further steps regarding the configuration of the FRUN Alert Management tool using an integration with external systems on the following page in the SAP Focused Run Expert Portal:

  • Alert Management - Integration with External Systems


Best regards,
Jessica
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg functionality not possible in Test Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are seeking an enhancement for the reordering or manual sequencing of Test Cases within a Test Plan in SAP Cloud ALM.  Some of our Test Plans have upwards of 50 Test Cases that must be completed in a specific sequence and the list of Test Cases must be shown in an order that we choose.

We’ve discovered on our own that we can manipulate the sequencing by manually and/or alphanumerically prefixing every Test Case title within the Test Plan, but we need a better solution than to repeatedly modify every Test Case title.

Does a different functionality already exists to sequence the Test Cases within the Test Plans?

 

Thanks,

 

PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg fun...
entry.content:
By default, the order test cases are assigned in a Test Plan is the order they are presented to the user.  The Test Plan application does offer a multi-level, user-controlled sort sequence based on Solution Process, Scope and/or Title -> check KBA 3610413 - How to re-order assigned Test Cases inside a Test Plan? - Cloud ALM

In case these offerings do not meet your sortation requirements, please feel free to log a request in the Influencer Campaign:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Using Hire.csv with Onboarding 2.0 and using the Standard Export
entry.content:

Is there a #SuccessFactors #KB or other resource we can reference for using #Hire.csv with #Onboarding2.0 and the #navigation required for the standard export?

Thanks,
David D'Angelo

entry.title: SAP Task Center with SAP Build Process Automation no Approve/Reject button on Mobile Start
entry.content:

I activated SAP Task Center connecting it to SAP Build Process Automation.

So far so good, but when I view the task on SAP Mobile Start it is not presenting the Approve/Reject buttons.

Does anyone know if it is a product limitation or if I am missing a configuration somewhere?

Regards,

Mauricio

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release: What are the highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development?
entry.content:
Following the successful launch of SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development in late 2024, we are excited to unveil new innovations and enhancements designed to benefit both managers and employees.

With AI-assisted successor insights, managers can now evaluate and compare named successors for a position more easily. Leveraging generative AI capabilities, succession planners can gain insights into the successors that are nominated for a position. In the succession org chart or the position tile view, users can leverage this new feature by selecting the generate insights button in the header of a position card. The insights provide a comprehensive overall summary section which offers a high-level overview of the job role requirements and all nominated successors. It is divided into two main parts, the role overview and successors analysis. A detailed summary is provided for each successor, encompassing three key aspects including experience and progression, performance and key achievements, and strengths and potential areas for growth.

AI-assisted successor insights

Managers can also now track their team's career development in a summary view, including career aspirations and skill growth in the latest version of career worksheet. Managers can access the summary view via the growth portfolio team view, where they find a comprehensive overview of their team's roles, aspirations, and skills. The 'Roles and Aspirations' section displays current job roles and the top three most popular target roles, including team readiness and employee details. In the 'Identify Skill Strengths' section, managers can see the top five skills and attributes where employees excel, track skill development over time, and filter by roles or critical skills. Similarly, the 'Recognize Skills to Improve' section highlights the top five skills and attributes where employees have gaps, offering the same tracking and filtering functionalities to support targeted skill development. These insights make it easier to monitor and guide team development and identify priorities for growth at the team level.

Team development insights

Additionally, we introduced new enhancements to the opportunity marketplace feature. The vetting process has been improved for assignment owners and co-owners when reviewing applicants with the ability to pause an assignment posting. This gives them greater flexibility in managing existing applications. Also, employees can now identify and apply for assignments across multiple locations, while HR personnel and managers can view opportunity marketplace assignments within the latest people profile, ensuring greater visibility into assignment status.

To read about the latest 1H innovations for our other talent solutions check out:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning
  • SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals
  • Talent intelligence hub from SAP SuccessFactors

Explore more innovations across the suite in the 1H 2025 release by checking out the release highlights brochure, overview video featuring Bianka Woelke and much more on the Product Release & Road Map Information page.

entry.title: SAP RAP Free Search and list sort in Fiori Application
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

While creating a sample RAP application from the SAP Learning course "Building Apps with the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model" 

I am caught with 2 issues

1. Free search is not working in the Fiori app. 

Images of BO projection

Fiori Application Data

 

with Free Search

 

 

2. Sorting Order of the List in the Fiori Application

Any advice that could help me understand why I am encountering the above issues would be greatly appreciated.

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model SAP Fiori SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release: What are the highlights for the talent intelligence hub?
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce our enhanced role-based permissions for the talent intelligence hub. This update gives administrators the ability to select by specific attribute type or tag which attributes should be visible and ratable. The attributes in the growth portfolio can be restricted from the employee view based on the target criteria that is set for a target population. This enhancement helps administrators decide who can see which attributes and improves support for organizations with complex permissions requirements.

Additionally, with our new validation workflow for the growth portfolio, you can define an approval process to be triggered whenever a change is made to the rating of an attribute. From the growth portfolio, when there is a change to the proficiency level for which a workflow is configured, the request is sent to the designated reviewers for approval. The reviewers are notified and can approve or reject the request from the workflow to-do cards. From the approval request details page, the reviewers can take action to approve or reject. This validation process boosts governance and enhances awareness of growth portfolio activities.

Talent intelligence hub workflow support

For learners, we are offering skills-focused learning in the growth portfolio with a curated list of learning opportunities to improve key skills and competencies. Users can discover filtered learning options tailored to their upskilling needs under the new 'Advance My Proficiency' section. With this enhancement, employees can choose the right courses to improve their skills and advance in their careers.

Skills-focused learning opportunities

Interested to learn best practices for getting started or gaining traction with the talent intelligence hub? Just us on June 4th to gain a clear, step-by-step approach to adoption. Click here to register. 

To read about the latest 1H innovations for our talent solutions check out:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development
  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning
  • SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Changing custom table delivery class from A to C
entry.content:
Hello Group!

I have a requirement to change the delivery class of a Z table from A to C (so entries can be transported). The table already exists in production and has data in it. 

Will the data in the target systems (production, quality) be deleted once the change in delivery class is moved?

Has anyone done this?

Thank you

Francisco

entry.title: Is ARIBA purchase requisitioning content in the SEN S/4 standard work area?
entry.content: Hi, I find the ARIBA sourcing content, but we are looking for the ARIBA purchase requisitioning process for cost center purchases for non-inventory items.  Does this content exist in the standard SAP S/4 workspace?

entry.title: Re: Can we remove "Send Reminder" button in Team View?
entry.content: @Thiru_V, yes, there is. The "Manager Reminders" permission would need to be removed from the user's role (Team View and Overdue Training Reminders). It'd remove the "Send Reminder" option from the Learning home page as well.

entry.title: Re: Streamline asset inspection process with Visual Asset Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Hi @james_loar,

you are absolutely right, there is only a value from the image and the measurements taken from it if you can link this to the technical object (in the train example the thickness of the contact stripes).
This is why our visual inspection scenarios require to send the image with the business context, as you can see from our API documentation https://api.sap.com/api/VisualInspection_APIs/path/post_InspectionResult You must provide at least the technical object ID, the technical object type (equipment or FLOC), SSID and the visual inspection view (e.g. you have different camera views or inspection to be executed on the same technical object, this helps you to map it to a certain inspection view).
So yes, that is exactly how it is done in APM, maybe the screenshot in the video doesn't show the asset information as we maximized the image and visual inspection result, but the business context is always given.

To you last point: yes, you can do this with CBM rules. Simply create a monitoring rule, which checks for all trains if the last timestamp from the indicator value which is used to store the thickness/height is older then 7 days and create an alert, user notification or maintenance notification. You can schedule this rule to run every day and then you will see which trains might need to be manual inspected next time they come to a service point.
I hope your concerns are all addressed, if not don't be shy an reach out to me directly.

BR
Philipp

entry.title: Re: Disable Windows Authentication on Data Source in RPT...
entry.content: Thanks, Don. I'll try using the RAS Assemblies, RDClientDoc, and ReplaceConnection. I did find our web app loads these assemblies. Dev is already planning to upgrade to 2020 later this year. Thanks for your help so far. Nice sample code you posted there. 🙂

entry.title: Re: CROSS PLANT STORAGE LOCATION GRN ISSUE FOR PRODUCTION...
entry.content:
Hello

In this case, special procurement 80 won't be the best solution. You should use a Stock Transfer special procurement type, so that MRP generates stock transfers and the production order in the production plant.

Regards,

Caetano

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Hi CKnall,

SAP Data Services standard offering for new customers is on SAP Private Cloud Edition (PCE). Existing customers under maintenance should be able to download in order to upgrade their respective environments. I would suggest to work with your SAP Account Manager to explore all the methods of Data Services offering platforms.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Customize request for class tab
entry.content: @ramyamula, the labels of the fields could be modified. You can follow the instructions of the KBA 2913764 to install and use the debug locale to find the label IDs. Ability to edit the "Request a Class" options is an enhancement - this is an open one that could be reviewed and voted on.

entry.title: Re: Bi weekly/Weekly Notifications for pending Time Sheet in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi @NeeleshKamath, I tried this solution but whenever I run IC job the flag for Is Time sheet submitted is automatically setting as No and notification goes out even after submitting timesheet. If I remove the variable timesheet not submitted at all then flag for Is Time sheet submitted is setting as "Yes" even if the time sheet is not submitted. Attached the rule that I have created. Can you please help what could be the possible reason for this behavior.

entry.title: Re: Learning Custom fields description Fill
entry.content: @luisfrancisco, not yet. There are some open enhancement requests about it. It'd be possible to review the enhancements and vote on the ones that fulfill your need.

entry.title: what's the frequency count of each part
entry.content: Is there a report or tile that will show me the frequency count for each part? We need proof for auditor that we are keeping up with the system generated cycle counts using ABC.

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Free Search and list sort in Fiori Application
entry.content:
@search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8

I think you are missing annotation for the search.

entry.title: Re: Inventory CDS View with S/4 HANA
entry.content: Thanks for sharing, Please do let me know if there is any CDS view available for the same data on S/4HANA Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Free Search and list sort in Fiori Application
entry.content: https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/49a6ba5b8d6946208322a9f7e16837c2 for sorting

entry.title: Orlando Wrapped, Madrid Awaits: Your SAP Sapphire 2025 Utilities Guide
entry.content:
The energy in Orlando was electric as SAP Sapphire 2025 kicked off and showcased how it's transforming enterprise operations through SAP Business AI, integrating applications, data, and AI to help businesses thrive amid uncertainty. Key innovations include new AI agents and the widespread availability of the AI co-pilot Joule, aimed at boosting user productivity by 30% and enabling faster, more confident decision-making.

For our sector, the utility-focused sessions were particularly inspiring. We extend our gratitude to our customers, including Loudoun Water, Vistra, SCE, NPPD, NextEra, Southwest Gas, People's Gas, and others, for sharing their invaluable project insights, outcomes, and experiences. These industry pioneers are shaping the future of energy and water, and we are privileged to support their transformation journeys.

On May 20th, Southern California Edison (SCE) showcased how utility companies can enhance customer experience through SAP Business Suite and SAP Signavio. In their presentation, Karen Cheung (SCE) and Jay Castleburry (SAP) emphasized the importance of strategic planning and demonstrated how Signavio helps uncover hidden opportunities—enabling small investments to yield significant returns. Their story stands as a compelling example of business-driven transformation.

 

 

Another standout attraction was the Learning & Certification Lab—a dynamic space where participants could test their skills through lightning-fast quizzes and experience how gamified learning transforms curiosity into real growth. It was a vivid reminder that learning is a continuous journey—and it’s even better when we grow together. #SkillUpwithSAP

Last but not least, Innovation is the driving force behind progress, and this was powerfully demonstrated through the inspiring and immersive industry showcases.

You missed Sapphire Orlando: Check the recordings on SAP Sapphire virtual.

What’s Coming in Madrid

As we shift gears to Madrid, expect a continuation of the momentum with a European twist.
Explore the solution tracks at SAP Sapphire in Madrid for the Utilities Industry, to learn how to bring your data and analytics, AI, application development, automation, and integration into one unified environment.
Have a look at our recommended sessions for Utilities in the SAP Sapphire session catalog to find what you are looking for.

 

Biggest value at in-person events: Gather with Peers

• Industries Expert Bar
Take the opportunity to meet 1:1 with our Utilities experts at the Industry Expert Bar – Hall 10, (IND1504)

• Utility Industry Roundtable with Daniela Haldy-Sellmann, VP, Head of Energy & Utilities Industries
Tuesday, May 27 @ 12:30pm – Hall 7, Room 7.400

Join the Utilities Session Highlights to learn from other utilities and SAP experts listed on the right. A special thank you to our customes for sharing their experience, like Saudi Electricity Company, UPM, X-ELIO and others...

 

 

Utilities Session Highlights

Day Two – Tuesday, May 27

  • Discover the industry-specific solution portfolio from SAP
    🕚11:30am – Hall 10, Theatre 11 (IND1138)
  • SAP’s cloud evolution: Integrating AI, security, and compliance
    🕐1:00pm – Hall 10, Theatre 18 (SER1370)
  • Achieving a clean core with your RISE with SAP journey
    🕐1:00pm – Hall 10, Theatre 17 (SER1211)
  • Transformation in the energy industry
    🕜1:30pm – Ask the Expert, 10.207 (IND1463)
  • Managing procurement transformation with Saudi Electricity Company and UPM
    🕜1:30pm – Hall 9, Theatre 10 (SPM1144)
  • Journeying to SAP Integration Suite and generative AI at National Grid
    🕜1:30pm – Hall 10, Theatre 17 (BTP1201)
  • From Strategy to Impact: How SAP Is Shaping the Next Era for Industries
    🕒3:00pm – Hall 9, Theatre 5 (IND1507)

Executive and Celebrity Keynotes

SAP Keynote: Your best. Made real. 

Tuesday, May 27
🕦8:30am Keynote Theater (KEY100)

Celebrity Keynote: How to lead and inspire change. With Jürgen Klopp

Wednesday, May 28
🕦1:00pm -Keynote Theater (KEY102)

Day Three – Wednesday, May 28

  • How X-ELIO transformed to cloud ERP with GROW with SAP
    🕘9:00am – Hall 9, Theatre 3 (ERP1235)
  • Leverage SAP Business AI to drive industry innovation and differentiation
    🕦11:30am – Hall 10, Theatre 16 (IND1137)

 

Whether you were in Orlando or are joining us in Madrid, SAP Sapphire 2025 is shaping the future of business.
We wish you an inspiring Sapphire Madrid experience!

entry.title: Integrate me.sap.com with ServiceNow
entry.content:
We have ServiceNow as main ticketing tool. We do have Solution Manager as well which is only used for Monitoring.

What we are doing currently is for any customer ( our customer ) raised any issues and we are seeking help from SAP and we raise incident on me.sap.com for our listed sap system. When SAP comes back on our incident with an update, our team has to go and check the me.sap.com. What we are trying to do is to check if we can integrate me.sap.com with our credential opens and integrates with servicenow and updates the incident raised in SNOW with an update so that we reduce the manual effort of cut copy paste from the SAP for me site and paste it in the appropriate incident raised for our customer in snow. 

Any one has any idea on this pls share

 

Best Regards

Rashit

entry.title: Re: Provisioning between MRP storage areas
entry.content: Hola, tengo configurado las areas MRP con almacenes de entradas y almacenes de produccion en el maestro del material en la vista MRP2 y la clase de aprovisionamiemto de TRASLADO y PRODUCCION en otro centro pero al ejecutar la MD02 solo me hace el aprovisionamiento desde la planta hacia el area MRP de almacenamiento y se necesita que sea desde el area MRP de almacenamiento de la planta A hacia el area de almacenamiento de la planta B
